windows.objective: Loss-tolerant precursor: prove EDL, power, pilot ISRU, ECLSS testbed
enables: 840 kWe of storm-proof power — roughly 700 homes' worth, with 27 kW of headroom over the installed load.
Batteries hold 4.9 MWh — 6 hours of full-base power with zero generation (dust storms are a non-event on fission).
Fuel plant produces ~1.3 t of methalox per day — a full Starship return load every 34 months.
483 t of propellant banked by the 2033-03 window — 0.3 Starship return flights' worth.
300 m³ of pressurized volume beyond the ships — long-duration habitable space for ~12 people.
Robot workforce: 10 humanoids and 2 rovers — the only crew on the planet.
EDL track record: landings so far demonstrate ~40% reliability (rule-of-three floor); this window's ships each land with ~50% odds.
302 t of hardware on the surface — 0.7× the mass of the ISS, and 0.03% of the ~1,000,000 t Musk estimates for a self-sufficient city.

windows.objective: Fuel factory x2: double rate-matched ISRU + the survival bridge, pre-landed
enables: 3,200 kWe of storm-proof power — roughly 2,667 homes' worth, with 54 kW of headroom over the installed load.
Batteries hold 18.9 MWh — 6 hours of full-base power with zero generation (dust storms are a non-event on fission).
Fuel plant produces ~5.9 t of methalox per day — a full Starship return load every 8 months.
2,926 t of propellant banked by the 2035-05 window — 2.1 Starship return flights' worth.
300 m³ of pressurized volume beyond the ships — long-duration habitable space for ~12 people.
Robot workforce: 20 humanoids and 4 rovers — the only crew on the planet.
EDL track record: landings so far demonstrate ~80% reliability (rule-of-three floor); this window's ships each land with ~62% odds.
934 t of hardware on the surface — 2.2× the mass of the ISS, and 0.09% of the ~1,000,000 t Musk estimates for a self-sufficient city.

windows.objective: First crew: 12 land with pilots for everything the village scales
enables: First crew lands at a base that is already powered, provisioned, and holds their return propellant.
Resident population: 12 — every per-capita clock (power, food area, habitat volume, imports) now has a denominator.
Agriculture: 12 modules can feed ~48 people (surplus vs 12 residents).
5,680 kWe of storm-proof power — roughly 4,733 homes' worth, with 86 kW of headroom over the installed load.
Batteries hold 33.6 MWh — 6 hours of full-base power with zero generation (dust storms are a non-event on fission).
Fuel plant produces ~7.6 t of methalox per day — a full Starship return load every 6 months.
7,067 t of propellant banked by the 2037-07 window — 5.0 Starship return flights' worth. The crew's ride home could be filled 5.0 times over.
1,500 m³ of pressurized volume beyond the ships — long-duration habitable space for ~60 people.
45 t of cached food/water/O2 — a crew of 12 could live on it for 25 months with zero recycling.
Robot workforce: 100 humanoids and 10 rovers.
EDL track record: landings so far demonstrate ~90% reliability (rule-of-three floor); this window's ships each land with ~74% odds.
Water independence: if mining stopped, ECLSS recycles ~90% and cached water covers the makeup for ~251 months (12 residents) — propellant production halts, the crew does not.
1,840 t of hardware on the surface — 4.4× the mass of the ISS, and 0.18% of the ~1,000,000 t Musk estimates for a self-sufficient city.

9,000 m³ of pressurized volume beyond the ships — long-duration habitable space for ~360 people.
345 t of cached food/water/O2 — a crew of 12 could live on it for 189 months with zero recycling.
Robot workforce: 160 humanoids and 18 rovers.
EDL track record: landings so far demonstrate ~95% reliability (rule-of-three floor); this window's ships each land with ~86% odds.
Water independence: if mining stopped, ECLSS recycles ~90% and cached water covers the makeup for ~206 months (112 residents) — propellant production halts, the crew does not.
3,974 t of hardware on the surface — 9.5× the mass of the ISS, and 0.40% of the ~1,000,000 t Musk estimates for a self-sufficient city.

rows.stmt: The first crew shall arrive at a powered, provisioned, and fueled Mars base with a demonstrated return path, and no single cargo-ship loss shall defeat the campaign.
rows.rationale: The program's definition of done for the pre-crew era.

rows.stmt: Starship shall reach orbit and be recovered.
rows.rationale: Nothing Mars-bound flies without orbital Starship; nearest gate.

rows.stmt: Both Starship stages shall be reused (routine refly).
Orbital propellant transfer (refill) shall be demonstrated.
rows.rationale: A Mars-bound ship launches nearly empty and refills in LEO; undemonstrated mid-2026.

rows.stmt: Long-duration cryogenic propellant storage (active chill) shall be demonstrated.
rows.rationale: Cryofuel must survive months in LEO and cruise; boil-off management unproven.

rows.stmt: The program shall demonstrate survivable EDL of a 100 t-class cargo ship on Mars.
rows.rationale: Heaviest object ever landed is ~1 t; 20-100x beyond demonstrated.

rows.stmt: At least 5 successful cargo landings shall be accumulated before crew commits.
rows.rationale: Statistical confidence in EDL beyond a single success.

rows.stmt: Follow-on ships shall land within meters of the base (precision landing aids operating).
The base shall have continuous, dust-storm-tolerant baseload power.
rows.rationale: Power is the tall pole; solar collapses in global storms.

rows.stmt: Installed generation shall reach 500 kWe or more in the first window.
rows.rationale: Pilot ISRU + margin; redundant fleet delivers ~640 kWe.

rows.stmt: Installed generation shall cover the delivered average load at every window.
rows.rationale: A power-starved plant silently derates ISRU production.

rows.stmt: Installed generation shall reach 1.5 MWe or more before crew arrival.
rows.rationale: Full-scale ISRU (~850 kW chain) plus habitat and margin.

rows.stmt: The program shall confirm and exploit accessible water ice (prospect, excavate, process).
The program shall produce and store a full crewed-return propellant load (1,400 t methalox) before crew commits.
rows.rationale: The single milestone that makes a crewed return credible; the deciding risk.

rows.stmt: An integrated pilot chain shall run at 40 kg/hr propellant-equivalent or more in the first window.
rows.rationale: Proves the full loop (capture-react-electrolyze-liquefy-store) at pilot scale.

rows.stmt: Chain nameplate rate shall reach one return load per synod (>= 112 kg/hr).
rows.rationale: The rate-matched fuel-factory buy (size_chain design point).

rows.stmt: Cumulative propellant produced and stored shall reach 1,400 t before crew departure.
At least two independent Sabatier chains shall exist on the surface before crew.
rows.rationale: Plant-level redundancy; a chain fault must not strand the crew.

rows.stmt: An uncrewed Mars-surface-to-Earth return demonstration shall depart before the first crew launches and shall complete before the crew's own return flight, and the crew's carried provisions plus the next synod's resupply shall cover a missed return window if the demonstration fails.
rows.rationale: L0-MSN-01 promises a demonstrated return path, but producing propellant (L1-PROP-01) is not flying it home. Synodic geometry gives exactly one useful slot: the demonstrator departs Mars ~Jan 2035 (on the doubled 2033 plant's surplus), reports at Earth ~Aug 2035 while the crew is in transit, and the crew's own ~Mar 2037 return flies a profile flown 19 months earlier. The accepted residual is the crew committing (May 2035) three months before the demo reports; if the demo fails and the crew must wait out a window for a fix, the hedge is their own one-synod provisions flown with them plus the next synod's (2037) resupply flight, which arrives before that supply runs out. Nothing consumable pre-lands ahead of the crew (decision 2026-07-21: the only prior pre-land, the survival bridge, was removed once consumables were dropped from the life_support_closed gate).

rows.stmt: An Earth-entry thermal protection system rated for ~11.6 km/s interplanetary-return entry (~8x the heat load of a ~5.7 km/s Mars arrival) shall be qualified.
rows.rationale: Return entry speed is ~2x Mars arrival and heating scales ~v^3; the return TPS is a distinct qualification item, not the Mars-EDL heat shield reused. This is the sharpest outbound/return asymmetry.

rows.stmt: Mars ascent and trans-Earth injection (~6.5 km/s from the surface, single stage, no Mars-orbit refueling) shall be qualified.
rows.rationale: Leaving Mars is far cheaper in delta-v than leaving Earth (~5.0 vs 11.2 km/s escape), but the vehicle must lift the entire return load off the surface on ISRU propellant, because there is no orbital tanker at Mars.

rows.stmt: Transfer and loading of ISRU-produced methalox into the return vehicle, and its cryogenic hold through the surface wait, shall be demonstrated; the return flight shall carry propellant samples for Earth-side verification.
rows.rationale: The propellant the whole architecture depends on must actually be loaded and kept flight-quality on the surface; bringing samples home closes the loop on the deciding risk with the lightest possible cargo.

rows.stmt: A full crewed-return propellant load shall be produced and stored before the uncrewed return flight, which consumes a real load.
rows.rationale: Ties the return-flight demonstration to actual propellant availability; the demonstrator burns ~1,400 t, which the 2033 factory banks by ~late 2034.

rows.stmt: A near-closed life-support loop shall be demonstrated on Mars for 1,000 sols before crew.
rows.rationale: ISS-class ECLSS without spares gives <1% mission reliability; demo runtime is the proof.

rows.stmt: The ECLSS demonstration article shall be landed in the first window (demo clock).
rows.rationale: 1,000 sols of runtime requires delivery no later than 2 windows before crew.

rows.stmt: The first crew shall fly with at least one synod of consumable provisions (food, water, oxygen); nothing consumable need be pre-landed ahead of them.
rows.rationale: Survival margin travels with the people, not ahead of them. Pre-landing was removed 2026-07-21 (see CONSIDERED and L1-RET-01): per-ship pairing already defeats independent ship loss, and a missed return window is covered by this carried supply plus the next synod's resupply. Three caches (~45 t) is one synod for twelve.

rows.stmt: The life-support demonstration shall run as an integrated article: the ECLSS loop operating inside a landed, pressurized habitat, both on the surface for the full 1,000-sol clock.
rows.rationale: A closed loop can only be demonstrated against a sealed volume, and the integrated failure modes (interface leaks, condensation on the bladder wall, dust in heat exchangers) are the ones that matter. The habitat itself also needs long-duration demonstration.

rows.stmt: Pressurized habitat volume beyond the landed ships shall be ready before crew.
At least 5 habitat modules (rigid or inflatable) shall be on the surface before crew arrival.
A storm shelter and radiation protection shall be in place before crew.
A surface-to-Earth communications link shall operate from the first window.
The base shall operate autonomously under a 4-22 minute one-way light delay.
Surface mobility for cargo and instruments shall be operational from the first window.
Cargo offload, power distribution, and site logistics shall be operational from the first window.
Every flight window's manifest shall fit within its declared fleet's mass capacity.
Every flight window shall retain at least 10% fleet mass margin for growth and late additions.
rows.rationale: Concept-stage hardware grows; AIAA practice suggests far more than our 10% contingency. A window at 100% booked has no room for reality.

rows.stmt: No single cargo-ship loss in any window shall cost a schedule-critical capability (power, water, propellant chain, comms, autonomy, infrastructure, mobility, precision landing), accounting for hardware already on the surface.
rows.rationale: Crew-era capabilities may accept single-hull risk (re-flight margin); schedule-critical ones may not.

findings: Window 0 retires 50% of weighted program risk — the EDL, power, water, autonomy and comms unknowns die on first landing.
life_support_closed (weight 15) retires at 2033-03 — schedule-critical; a slip here slips first crew.
return_propellant_proven (weight 25) retires at 2033-03 — schedule-critical; a slip here slips first crew.
eclss_habitat: 1 unit(s) at 2031-01 counted as demonstration article(s) — early delivery here is deliberate risk buy-down, not idle mass.
habitat_inflatable: 1 unit(s) at 2031-01 counted as demonstration article(s) — early delivery here is deliberate risk buy-down, not idle mass.
No idle crew-era mass beyond verification margin — manifests are lean.
